results.rendered_html: upa-cāra—cchala n. a kind of fallacious inference (to be refuted by reference to the real sense of a word used metaphorically, e.g. if any one from the sentence ‘the platform cries’ were to conclude that the platform really cries and not persons on the platform),  Nyāyad. ;  Nyāyak.
